title: Umami Crispy Malaysian Flour Stir-fry
description: A quick and satisfying post-workout meal featuring a rich blend of umami flavors and crispy white flour, perfect for those following a fodmap-friendly diet.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup gluten-free flour mix
ingredients.notes: Use low FODMAP variety if necessary

ingredients.name: 1 small onion, finely chopped
ingredients.notes: Can be omitted for a thinner sauce or smoother texture

ingredients.name: 1/4 teaspoon fish sauce (optional)
ingredients.notes: For a savory umami boost, use low sodium or FODMAP-friendly variety

instructions: Mix the flour with salt and pepper in a shallow dish.

Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat. Dip each piece of gluten-free flour mix into the flour mixture, coat evenly on both sides, then fry for about 30 seconds until golden and crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In the same pan, add remaining 2 tablespoons of oil, followed by chopped onion (if using) and minced garlic. Sauté until fragrant, around 1-2 minutes.
Pour in soy sauce and lime juice, stirring well to combine all ingredients. Add optional fish sauce for extra umami flavor if desired.
Once your tofu/fry mix is ready, stir-fry it into the sauce mixture until well coated and heated through.
Spoon the sauce over the crispy flour pieces and toss gently to coat evenly. Serve immediately while hot.
